heading: Reports to tribes
label: 2809 Reports to tribes
notes: References in TextRule 6 of the Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure, referred to in subsec. (c)(2), is set out in the Appendix to Title 18, Crimes and Criminal Procedure.

Amendments2010— added subsecs. (a) to (c) and struck out former subsecs. (a) to (d) which related, respectively, to reports by law enforcement officials of the Bureau of Indian Affairs or Federal Bureau of Investigation, reports by United States attorneys, case files included within reports, and transfer or disclosure of confidential or privileged communication, information, or sources to tribal officials.Pub. L. 111–211
amendments: Amendments2010— added subsecs. (a) to (c) and struck out former subsecs. (a) to (d) which related, respectively, to reports by law enforcement officials of the Bureau of Indian Affairs or Federal Bureau of Investigation, reports by United States attorneys, case files included within reports, and transfer or disclosure of confidential or privileged communication, information, or sources to tribal officials.Pub. L. 111–211

children.heading: Investigative coordination
children.label: 1 Investigative coordination
children.text: Subject to subsection (c), if a law enforcement officer or employee of any Federal department or agency terminates an investigation of an alleged violation of Federal criminal law in Indian country without referral for prosecution, the officer or employee shall coordinate with the appropriate tribal law enforcement officials regarding the status of the investigation and the use of evidence relevant to the case in a tribal court with authority over the crime alleged.

children.label: B
children.text: the statuses of the accused as Indians or non-Indians;

children.label: C
children.text: the statuses of the victims as Indians or non-Indians; and

children.label: D
children.text: the reasons for deciding against referring the investigation for prosecution.

children.heading: Prosecutorial coordination
children.label: 3 Prosecutorial coordination
children.text: Subject to subsection (c), if a United States Attorney declines to prosecute, or acts to terminate prosecution of, an alleged violation of Federal criminal law in Indian country, the United States Attorney shall coordinate with the appropriate tribal justice officials regarding the status of the investigation and the use of evidence relevant to the case in a tribal court with authority over the crime alleged.

children.label: B
children.text: the statuses of the accused as Indians or non-Indians;

children.label: C
children.text: the statuses of the victims as Indians or non-Indians; and

children.label: D
children.text: the reasons for deciding to decline or terminate the prosecutions.

children.label: i
children.text: for the Federal Bureau of Investigation, by Field Division; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for United States Attorneys, by Federal judicial district; and

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Nothing in this section requires any Federal agency or official to transfer or disclose any confidential, privileged, or statutorily protected communication, information, or source to an official of any Indian tribe.

children.heading: Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure
children.label: 2 Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure
children.text: Nothing in this section affects or limits the requirements of Rule 6 of the Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 3 Regulations
children.text: The Attorney General shall establish, by regulation, standards for the protection of the confidential or privileged communications, information, and sources described in this section.
